chapter_title.en: Chapter on Establishing Ihram, Its Conditions, Nullification, and the Prayer for it

text: 2569 - وَ كَتَبَ بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِنَا إِلَى أَبِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ دَخَلَ مَسْجِدَ اَلشَّجَرَةِ فَصَلَّى وَ أَحْرَمَ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ مِنَ اَلْمَسْجِدِ فَبَدَا لَهُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ أَ لَهُ أَنْ يَنْقُضَ ذَلِكَ بِمُوَاقَعَةِ اَلنِّسَاءِ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «نَعَمْ» أَوْ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2569 - One of our companions wrote to Abu Ibrahim (as) regarding a man who entered Masjid al-Shajarah, prayed, and entered Ihram. Then, before reciting the Talbiyah, he left the mosque and decided to nullify his Ihram by engaging in intercourse with women.
Imam (as) wrote in reply: "Yes" or "There is no harm in it."
